set.body:
CRAN accepts on first submission. Users understand what's supported, what's fast, what's experimental.

Tasks:
1. Second vignette 'When is amatrix fast?': per-op crossover points referenced to calibration output from Track 4. Users need to know when NOT to use amatrix
2. Backend compatibility matrix in README: generated from audit script (Track 3) + tier evidence (Track 5). Backend <c3><97> op <c3><97> status (<e2><9c><93> / <e2><97><8b> experimental / <e2><80><94>). Cannot drift by hand
3. Error message audit: review every stop() and warning() in R/ for (a) what went wrong (b) what the user did (c) what to do next. One afternoon, huge UX payoff
4. NEWS.md for 0.1.0: hand-written, user-facing, not a changelog dump. 'What works, what's fast, what's experimental, what's new'
5. R CMD check --as-cran clean on mac + winbuilder + rhub (R-release + R-devel)
6. revdepcheck::revdep_check() and note result in cran-comments.md
7. R-universe verification post-CRAN via r-pkgdown-deploy skill

Verification: submission sent, winbuilder + rhub reports green, R-universe build green.

Plan file: /Users/bbuchsbaum/.claude/plans/glistening-hopping-squid.md Track 7.
set.title: Hardening Track 7: Documentation, CRAN, and Release
